teaching_card.title: Verb Tense and Form (Easy)
teaching_card.body: Match the verb to the time the sentence describes. Clues like 'last year' signal past, 'every day' or 'currently' signal present, and 'next year' or 'will' signal future. Keep tenses consistent.
key_points: Let time markers set the tense.
Past: -ed; present: base/-s; future: will + base.
An -ing form alone can't be the main verb.
teaching_card.worked_example: Last year, the team traveled (not travels) abroad.

screens.title: Match the time
screens.body: Pick the tense the sentence's time clues call for: 'last year,' 'in 1990' -> past; 'today,' 'currently' -> present; 'next year,' 'will' -> future. Don't switch times without a reason.
key_points: Keep tense consistent unless the meaning changes.

screens.title: Forms that trip people up
screens.body: An -ing word by itself can't be the main verb ('The team winning' is not a sentence). Use perfect tenses for one action before another: 'had finished' (before a past point), 'has finished' (up to now).
key_points: had + verb = earlier past; has/have + verb = up to now.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: 'Last spring the crew (plants/planted) trees.' 'Last spring' is past, so 'planted.' 'By the time we arrived, the show (started/had started)' needs 'had started' — it happened first.
